version.description: version is the manifest schema version (e.g., "1.0"). Used for forward-compatible parsing of manifest files.

metadata.description: metadata contains identifying information about this manifest.

instruments.description: instruments defines the asset types available to this tenant.

accountTypes.description: account_types defines the account classifications available to this tenant.

valuationRules.description: valuation_rules defines how instruments are converted between each other.

sagas.description: sagas defines the workflow definitions available to this tenant.

seedData.description: seed_data contains tenant-specific reference data (e.g., default parties, initial chart of accounts, industry-specific lookups). Uses google.protobuf.Struct for flexible JSONB representation.

paymentRails.description: payment_rails defines external payment provider integrations for this tenant.

partyTypes.description: party_types defines the tenant-configurable party type schemas for this tenant.

Manifest.title:
========================================
 Manifest Root
 ========================================
Manifest.description: ======================================== Manifest Root ========================================  Manifest is the atomic configuration snapshot for a Meridian tenant. It declares the complete business model: instruments, account types, valuation rules, and saga workflows. A manifest is self-contained with no external dependencies - all scripts are inline strings and all references resolve within the manifest itself. Codes (instrument codes, account type codes) are immutable primary keys. To change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

code.description: code is the unique, immutable identifier for this account type (e.g., "CURRENT", "SAVINGS", "CARBON_INVENTORY"). Must be uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, 1-50 characters.

name.description: name is the human-readable display name (e.g., "Current Account").

normalBalance.title:
========================================
 Account Type Definitions
 ========================================
normalBalance.description: ======================================== Account Type Definitions ========================================  NormalBalance defines the natural balance direction for an account type.

allowedInstruments.description: allowed_instruments lists the instrument codes that this account type can hold. Each value must match a code from the manifest's instruments list. Empty list means all instruments defined in the manifest are allowed.

policies.description: policies contains CEL expressions governing this account type's behavior.

meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypeDef.title: Account Type Definition
meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypeDef.description: AccountTypeDefinition declares an account classification within the manifest. The code field is the immutable primary key - to change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

validation.description: validation is a CEL expression evaluated before allowing transactions on this account type. Example: "amount > 0 && amount <= 1000000" Empty string means no additional validation beyond standard checks.

bucketing.description: bucketing is a CEL expression that determines how amounts are grouped into fungibility buckets. Example: "instrument_code + ':' + attributes.batch_id" Empty string means all amounts are fully fungible (single default bucket).

meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypePol.title: Account Type Policies
meridian.control_plane.v1.AccountTypePol.description: AccountTypePolicies contains CEL expressions that govern account behavior. CEL syntax validation is deferred to the runtime validator - the manifest only stores the expression strings.

code.description: code is the unique, immutable identifier for this instrument (e.g., "GBP", "KWH", "CARBON_CREDIT"). Must be uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, 1-50 characters.

name.description: name is the human-readable display name (e.g., "British Pound Sterling").

type.title:
========================================
 Instrument Definitions
 ========================================
type.description: ======================================== Instrument Definitions ========================================  InstrumentType classifies the broad category of an instrument.

dimensions.description: dimensions describes the unit and precision of this instrument's quantities.

meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDefi.title: Instrument Definition
meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDefi.description: InstrumentDefinition declares an asset type within the manifest. The code field is the immutable primary key - to change a code, delete the old definition and create a new one.

unit.description: unit is the display unit for this instrument (e.g., "GBP", "kWh", "TONNE_CO2E").

precision.description: precision is the number of decimal places for this instrument (0-18).

meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDime.title: Instrument Dimensions
meridian.control_plane.v1.InstrumentDime.description: InstrumentDimensions describes the unit and precision of an instrument's quantities.

name.description: name is the human-readable name for this manifest (e.g., "Acme Energy Trading").

industry.description: industry describes the target industry vertical (e.g., "energy", "banking", "carbon").

description.description: description provides additional context about this manifest's purpose.

meridian.control_plane.v1.ManifestMetada.title:
========================================
 Manifest Metadata
 ========================================
meridian.control_plane.v1.ManifestMetada.description: ======================================== Manifest Metadata ========================================  ManifestMetadata contains identifying information about a manifest.

mode.title: Connect Mode
mode.description: ConnectMode defines the Stripe Connect account type.

accountId.description: account_id is the Stripe Connect account identifier (format: acct_xxx).

webhookEndpointSecret.description: webhook_endpoint_secret is a reference to the webhook signing secret stored in a secret manager (e.g., "sm://stripe/webhook_secret"). Never contains the plaintext secret.

platformFee.description: platform_fee defines the fee structure for transactions on this rail.

payoutSchedule.title: Payout Schedule
payoutSchedule.description: PayoutSchedule defines when connected account payouts are initiated.

supportedMethods.description: supported_methods lists the payment methods enabled on this rail (e.g., "card", "sepa_debit", "bank_account").

meridian.control_plane.v1.PaymentRails.title: Payment Rails
meridian.control_plane.v1.PaymentRails.description: PaymentRails defines an external payment provider integration. Currently supports Stripe Connect for payment processing.

type.title:
========================================
 Payment Rails
 ========================================
type.description: ======================================== Payment Rails ========================================  PlatformFeeType defines how the platform fee is calculated.

value.description: value is the fee amount as a decimal string. For PERCENTAGE: "2.5" means 2.5%. For FLAT: "0.30" means 0.30 in the transaction currency. Must be a valid positive decimal value.

meridian.control_plane.v1.PlatformFee.title: Platform Fee
meridian.control_plane.v1.PlatformFee.description: PlatformFee defines the fee structure for payment rail transactions.

name.description: name is the unique identifier for this saga (e.g., "process_energy_settlement"). Must be lowercase alphanumeric with underscores.

trigger.description: trigger defines what initiates this saga. Supported prefixes:   "api:" - triggered by an API call (e.g., "api:/v1/settlements")   "webhook:" - triggered by an external webhook (e.g., "webhook:stripe_payment")   "scheduled:" - triggered on a schedule (e.g., "scheduled:daily_reconciliation")

script.description: script is the inline Starlark source code defining the saga workflow. Must not be empty - all saga logic is defined inline in the manifest. Starlark guarantees termination (no while loops, no recursion).

meridian.control_plane.v1.SagaDefinition.title:
========================================
 Saga Definitions
 ========================================
meridian.control_plane.v1.SagaDefinition.description: ======================================== Saga Definitions ========================================  SagaDefinition declares a workflow within the manifest. Sagas are triggered by API calls, webhooks, or scheduled events, and contain inline Starlark scripts that orchestrate service interactions.

fromInstrument.description: from_instrument is the source instrument code for conversion. Must match a code from the manifest's instruments list.

toInstrument.description: to_instrument is the target instrument code for conversion. Must match a code from the manifest's instruments list.

method.title:
========================================
 Valuation Rules
 ========================================
method.description: ======================================== Valuation Rules ========================================  ValuationMethod defines how instrument conversion rates are determined.

source.description: source identifies the rate source for this rule. For SPOT_RATE: the feed identifier (e.g., "ecb_fx_daily", "nordpool_spot"). For FIXED: a description of who sets the rate (e.g., "admin_override", "contract_rate").

meridian.control_plane.v1.ValuationRule.title: Valuation Rule
meridian.control_plane.v1.ValuationRule.description: ValuationRule defines how one instrument is converted to another. Used for cross-instrument transactions (e.g., kWh to GBP, USD to EUR).

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this party type definition.

tenantId.description: tenant_id is the tenant that owns this party type definition.

partyType.description: party_type is the party classification this definition applies to (e.g., "PERSON", "ORGANIZATION"). Uppercase alphanumeric with underscores, max 100 characters.

attributeSchema.description: attribute_schema is a JSON Schema document defining the structure and constraints for party attributes of this type (max 16KB). Must be non-empty when provided.

validationCel.description: validation_cel is a CEL expression for cross-field validation of party attributes. Evaluated at party registration and update time.

eligibilityCel.description: eligibility_cel is a CEL expression for determining account type eligibility for parties of this type.

errorMessageCel.description: error_message_cel is a CEL expression for generating custom error messages when validation or eligibility checks fail.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this party type definition was created.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this party type definition was last modified.

meridian.party.v1.PartyTypeDefinition.title: --- Party Type Definitions ---
meridian.party.v1.PartyTypeDefinition.description: --- Party Type Definitions ---  PartyTypeDefinition defines a tenant-configurable schema for a party type, including JSON Schema for attribute validation and CEL expressions for cross-field validation, account eligibility, and custom error messages.
